scope.note: APG/ERG parity is explicitly out of scope. guidance_interval=1.0 is the minimal sampling-side change required for captured-v parity (cond-only segments at out-of-interval steps would otherwise mismatch predict_velocity's always-mix semantic).

fixes_applied_relative_to_first_parity_r.fix: predict_velocity timestep dtype changed from model_dtype (bfloat16) to torch.float32 to match upstream scheduler.timesteps
sample() called with extras={'guidance_interval': 1.0} so CFG mixing applies to the entire trajectory (matches predict_velocity's stateless always-mix contract)

per_case_pattern: All 4 prompts × 3 seeds × σ {0.491, 0.329, 0.104} returned max_abs=0.0, mean_abs=0.0, rel=0.0 (bit-identical).

verdict.Q1_H5_correct: yes (confirmed against pipeline_ace_step.py main inference loop; no other upstream path produces v_cond at out-of-interval steps under these settings)
verdict.Q2_H1_dtype_fix_minimal: yes (float32 only affects sinusoidal time_proj precision; transformer.decode internally casts to hidden_states.dtype before the learned embedding path)
verdict.Q3_minimal_action: Option C (document constraint + Phase B scripts set guidance_interval=1.0 explicitly). Option A would silently change sampling trajectory; Option B adds required step_index/interval state
verdict.Q4_phase_b1_semantic_consistency: yes (Phase B.1 should use guidance_interval=1.0; with default 0.5, captured v_out is branch-dependent: mixed inside [start, end), v_cond outside)

phase_b1_implication: Phase B.1 reliability calibration uses captured v_out directly (not predict_velocity). With guidance_interval=0.5 default, captured v_out semantics differ across σ checkpoints depending on which side of the interval boundary they fall. For consistent 'full-CFG velocity' Tweedie reconstructions across all σ checkpoints, Phase B.1 sampling must explicitly set guidance_interval=1.0. This is a constraint to encode in the Phase B.1 config (configs/runs/phase_b1_reliability.yaml when authored); NOT applied to AceStepModel.sample() default yet (PI to decide if Option A is preferred).
